notes: Runs faster than on Windows, more stable as well!
title: 7 Days to Die

notes: Because in terms of performance the Linux version is garbage. But runs very nice with Proton and DXVK
title: 7 Days to Die

notes: Runs much better than native since A17 was released. Can set many of the video settings to max/ultra without issue.
title: 7 Days to Die

notes: Manual Proton selection confirmed. I played a standard fresh game on navezgane for a few hours at Max settings. Experimented here and there with combat, weapons and destruction. Performance was not only more responsive than in Windows 10, but my frames were consistently solid whereas in windows I would experience the occasional dip. I'll probably play 7DTD exclusively on Linux now.
title: 7 Days to Die

notes: Phenomenal performance. Even better performance than native and even Windows! My GPU usage was able to get up to 99%; can't say the same for the other two options. On almost ultra preset (ultra preset but with trees turned down to High), I experienced constant 60fps in open areas, and in urban areas, 45-60fps on average with quick drops to 30-40fps, especially when loading in things. I also tested with 4.11-2 to see if there was a performance gain, but the results were a mixed bag.
title: 7 Days to Die

notes:
150+ hours of Alpha 17.4 via DXVK and Alpha 16.4 via D9VK, no issues at all.
Native clients are not good, Proton vastly superior.
Excellent performance, utterly stable, cannot fault it. Using Mesa/ACO.
Single Player only tested.
title: 7 Days to Die
